errors: ZAP active scan timed out — partial results collected.

vulnerabilities.name: Outdated Apache Version (2.4.49)
vulnerabilities.description: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 is outdated and vulnerable to CVE-2021-41773 (path traversal / RCE).
vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade Apache to version 2.4.51 or later immediately. This vulnerability has public exploits available.

vulnerabilities.name: Open Port 23/TCP (Telnet)
vulnerabilities.description: Telnet service is running on port 23. All communication including credentials is transmitted in plaintext.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable Telnet immediately and replace with SSH (port 22) with key-based authentication.

vulnerabilities.name: Open Port 21/TCP (FTP)
vulnerabilities.description: FTP service detected on port 21. FTP transmits credentials and data in cleartext.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable FTP and replace with SFTP or SCP. If FTP is required, enforce FTPS and restrict access by IP.

vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection
vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability detected in the 'id' parameter. An attacker could extract, modify, or delete database content.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace all string-concatenated SQL with parameterised queries or prepared statements. Apply least-privilege DB accounts.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected)
vulnerabilities.description: Reflected XSS was detected in the 'search' parameter. User-supplied input is reflected in the response without proper encoding.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement context-sensitive output encoding for all user-controlled data. Apply a Content Security Policy.

vulnerabilities.name: Exposed .git Directory
vulnerabilities.description: The .git directory is publicly accessible, potentially exposing source code, credentials and deployment secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Block access to .git at the web server level. Rotate any exposed credentials immediately.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: Content-Security-Policy
vulnerabilities.description: No CSP header found. Without a Content Security Policy, the application has no XSS mitigation at the browser level.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Define and deploy a Content-Security-Policy header. Start with 'default-src https:' and tighten iteratively.

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Security Header: Strict-Transport-Security
vulnerabilities.description: HSTS header is not configured. The site is vulnerable to protocol downgrade attacks and SSL stripping.

vulnerabilities.name: Directory Listing Enabled
vulnerabilities.description: Directory listing is enabled on the web server, exposing internal file structure to unauthenticated users.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable directory listing: add 'Options -Indexes' in Apache or 'autoindex off;' in Nginx.

vulnerabilities.name: Cookie Without HttpOnly Flag
vulnerabilities.description: Session cookie 'PHPSESSID' is set without the HttpOnly flag, making it accessible to JavaScript and vulnerable to XSS-based session hijacking.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict to all session cookie Set-Cookie headers.

vulnerabilities.name: Server Version Disclosure via Server Header
vulnerabilities.description: The Server response header reveals: 'Apache/2.4.49 (Ubuntu)'. Version disclosure aids targeted attacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Configure 'ServerTokens Prod' in Apache or 'server_tokens off;' in Nginx to suppress version info.

vulnerabilities.name: Open Port 443/TCP (HTTPS)
vulnerabilities.description: HTTPS service running on port 443. Standard expected port — verify TLS configuration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Ensure TLS 1.2 or 1.3 only, disable weak cipher suites, and enable HSTS.
